rulings.Case Title: GARY STRENG VS. KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Condition Of Def Robert Camozzi
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 1: Plaintiff GARY STRENG's Motion for Order Permitting Discovery of Financial Condition of Defendant Robert Camozzi DENIED. Plaintiff seeks discovery responses due after discovery cut off date. Plaintiff's request for trial subpoena made in reply is denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND B SIMMONS VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 2: Defendant AMERESCO, INC.'S Demurer to Second Amended Complaint Sustained without leave to amend. Taking all allegations in the Second Amend Complaint as true Ameresco's alleged actions do not rise to the requisite level of outrageousness to support a cause of action fro Intentional infliction of Emotional Distress. Also, no facts are alleged showing intent/reckless disregard to cause emotional distress. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRIANNA MANNION et al VS. JOHN E. WAI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 3: Plaintiffs BRIANNA MANNION'S and TRAVIS LARIMER'S Motion for OCS Re: Contempt DENIED. All documents have been produced as of the date of the hearing. Documents on file are not redacted. No sanctions. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GOOEY "GEORGE" OCK ENG VS. ALICE EUBANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Alice Eubank'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 8: Defendant ALICE EUBANK'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Denied. (CCP § 437c.) Defendant has not demonstrated that there are no triable issues of fact. First, for each cause of action, Ms. Eubanks has not demonstrated that the statute of limitations bars each cause of action because she fails to address her alleged acts of conversion and fraud in May and June 2011. (Complaint, 18, 22 - 24.) Plaintiff Gooey "George" Ock Eng filed this action on May 17, 2013, which is within two years of these alleged acts. Second, for first cause of action, Plaintiff has not proffered material facts in support of all of the grounds stated in the Notice of Motion. (Notice of Motion, p. 2:12-14, See UMF nos. 1 - 20.) Third, for the second cause of action, Defendant fails to address all allegations of fraud and concealment. (Complaint, 39(e).) Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied as procedurally improper; Defendant failed to repeat verbatim the grounds stated in the Notice of Motion in her Separate Statement. (CRC 3.1350(b).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GOOEY "GEORGE" OCK ENG VS. ALICE EUBANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Jimmy Eng And Judy Jong'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 8: Cross-Defendants's JIMMY ENG'S and JUDY JONG'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Denied. First, the Notice of Motion is procedurally deficient (CRC 3.1110(a).) Cross-Defendants failed to state the grounds for relief in the first paragraph. Second, Cross-Defendants do not address the fourth, fifth, seventh, eleventh, and twelfth causes of action and have not demonstrated that there are no triable issues of fact as to the causes of action. Cross-Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is denied as procedurally improper; Cross-Defendants failed to repeat verbatim the grounds stated in the Notice of Motion in their Separate Statement. (CRC 3.1350(b).) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSELLA KENNEDY VS. WILLIAM KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT WILLIAM KENNEDY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS GRANTED without leave to amend. Notice purportedly given under CC 1946 is fatally defective as it does not comply with the requirements regarding abandoned property. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ptlf'S Responses To Discovery, And For An Order Establishing Admissions, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 5: Defendant's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Discovery, And For An Order Establishing Admissions, And Request For Sanctions GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide responses by hand delivery without objections to Form Interrogatories (Set One, Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Demand for Production (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $975. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BANKS VS. KEN MASSIE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT CYNTHIA SNOWDEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges Unlawful Detainer based on breach of covenant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BANKS VS. KEN MASSIE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT CYNTHIA SNOWDEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED, 5 days to answer. No authority is cited for the proposition that portions of the exhibits to complaint quoting terms of the underlying lease should be stricken under CCP 435 as false, irrelevant or improper. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL P. SWEENEY VS. CARLA CLYNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, March 17, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT ESTHER HERNANDEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of the requisite notice and payment of the relocation fees before and/or at the time of the service of the operative Notice. = (501/REQ)
